title: Getting started with PARAMETRA
text: PARAMETRA R data package containing curated parameters livestock disease modelling. package includes one combined dataset, parametra_long, plus one dataset per parameter group.

title: Getting started with PARAMETRA
code: # Install from GitHub # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("BIOSECURE-EU/parametra") library(parametra) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(tidyr)

title: Getting started with PARAMETRA
text: main dataset parametra_long, stacks parameter-group tables single analysis-ready table. column parameter_type identifies original parameter group. quick overview number records parameter group: number records pathogen:
code: parametra_long %>%   count(parameter_type, sort = TRUE) ## # A tibble: 8 × 2 ##   parameter_type                      n ##   <chr>                           <int> ## 1 transmission                     1076 ## 2 diagnostic_test                   721 ## 3 infectious_latent_incuba_period   420 ## 4 within_herd_prevalence            175 ## 5 regional_prevalence                92 ## 6 pathogen_survival                  48 ## 7 other                              22 ## 8 control_plan                        9 parametra_long %>%   count(pathogen, sort = TRUE) ## # A tibble: 24 × 2 ##    pathogen                   n ##    <chr>                  <int> ##  1 Avian Influenza          577 ##  2 Paratuberculosis         313 ##  3 E. coli                  300 ##  4 Hepatitis E              248 ##  5 African Swine Fever      186 ##  6 Bovine Tuberculosis      177 ##  7 PRRS                     120 ##  8 Swine Influenza          109 ##  9 Salmonella               102 ## 10 Foot and Mouth Disease    79 ## # ℹ 14 more rows

title: Getting started with PARAMETRA
text: analyses start filtering parametra_long. example, code finds African swine fever transmission records available numeric value. Use distinct() see values available filtering:
code: asf_transmission <- parametra_long %>%   filter(     pathogen == "African Swine Fever",     parameter_type == "transmission",     !is.na(value)   ) parametra_long %>%   distinct(parameter_type, parameter) %>%   arrange(parameter_type, parameter) %>%   head(20) ## # A tibble: 20 × 2 ##    parameter_type                  parameter                                     ##    <chr>                           <chr>                                         ##  1 control_plan                    NA                                            ##  2 diagnostic_test                 Sensitivity                                   ##  3 diagnostic_test                 Specificity                                   ##  4 infectious_latent_incuba_period Incubation period                             ##  5 infectious_latent_incuba_period Infectious period                             ##  6 infectious_latent_incuba_period Latent period                                 ##  7 infectious_latent_incuba_period Other                                         ##  8 infectious_latent_incuba_period Shape                                         ##  9 other                           Other                                         ## 10 pathogen_survival               Fomites transmission                          ## 11 pathogen_survival               Survival/Disinfection                         ## 12 regional_prevalence             Global Prevalence                             ## 13 regional_prevalence             Herd prevalence                               ## 14 regional_prevalence             Other                                         ## 15 transmission                    Basic reproduction number                     ## 16 transmission                    Other                                         ## 17 transmission                    Probability of reactivation of latent infect… ## 18 transmission                    Probability of transmission between farms     ## 19 transmission                    Probability of transmission via direct conta… ## 20 transmission                    Probability of transmission via indirect con…

what: Example 1: Transmission parameters for African swine fever
title: Getting started with PARAMETRA
text: example plots transmission-parameter values African swine fever, faceted parameter. record id kept plotting data source row can traced back PARAMETRA.
code: asf_transmission %>%   ggplot(aes(x = value, y = host)) +   geom_point(color = "#0F9DA4", size = 2.5, alpha = 0.8) +   facet_wrap(~ parameter, ncol = 1, scales = "free_x") +   labs(     title = "African swine fever transmission parameters",     subtitle = "Each point is one PARAMETRA record",     x = "Value",     y = "Host"   ) +   theme_minimal()

what: Example 2: Comparing infectious periods across pathogens
title: Getting started with PARAMETRA
text: compare infectious-period estimates three pathogens. example useful checking range values selecting parameters model.
code: infectious_periods <- parametra_long %>%   filter(     parameter == "Infectious period",     pathogen %in% c("Foot and Mouth Disease", "African Swine Fever", "Avian Influenza"),     !is.na(value)   ) ggplot(infectious_periods, aes(x = pathogen, y = value, fill = pathogen)) +   geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.4) +   geom_jitter(width = 0.15, height = 0, alpha = 0.7, size = 1.5) +   labs(     title = "Infectious-period estimates across pathogens",     x = NULL,     y = "Infectious period"   ) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "none")

title: Getting started with PARAMETRA
text: record includes reference fields parameter values can traced source. Useful columns include: ref: DOI, DOI URL, PubMed URL, stable web URL ref_short: short human-readable citation, available ref_status: status assigned PARAMETRA curation ref_last_access: date URL reference last checked.
code: parametra_long %>%   count(ref_status, sort = TRUE) ## # A tibble: 3 × 2 ##   ref_status        n ##   <chr>         <int> ## 1 doi_found      2530 ## 2 url_unchecked    32 ## 3 doi_not_found     1

title: Getting started with PARAMETRA
text: PARAMETRA curated support disease-modelling work, users still assess whether record appropriate specific modelling context. accepting parameter value suitable, recommend reviewing contextual information notes provided database, consulting original reference.

title: Getting started with PARAMETRA
text: New records can submitted PARAMETRA submission form via contact@parametra.eu.

title: Livestock Disease Transmission Parameters Database
text: curated database animal disease modelling, contains data infectious periods, transmission rates, pathogen survival, diagnostic tests, prevalence, control measures multiple livestock diseases. Originally developed BIOSECURE consortium semi-systematic literature review. information methodology, see accompanying publication: Antonopoulos, ., Ciria, N., Regan, Á., Tubay, J., Ciaravino, G., Hayes, B., Lambert, S., Vergne, T., Velkers, F., Biebaut, E., Viltrop, ., Dewulf, J., Charlier, J., Fischer, E., & Palau, . . (2025). PARAMETRA: transmission modelling database livestock diseases. Preventive Veterinary Medicine, 245, 106668. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.prevetmed.2025.106668 database continues grow thanks user contributions via PARAMETRA submission form. availability parameters pathogen, parameter type, year study type can visualized .

title: Livestock Disease Transmission Parameters Database
text: Click download parametra Excel file. file can also found data-raw/parametra.xlsx.

title: Livestock Disease Transmission Parameters Database
text: Access database individual sheets directly R programming environments livestock disease transmission modeling. Download CSV files R Navigate CSV file data-raw/tables Click raw top right Copy link (starting https://raw.githubusercontent.com/..) Example: Download parametra_long.csv (containing parameter tables) directly R: Python:

title: Livestock Disease Transmission Parameters Database
text: PARAMETRA entry contains parameter, pathogen, reference. Additional information varies parameter type may include host species, study type, value, upper lower confidence intervals. organized following tables: transmission: Reproduction number, transmission parameter, probability infection given direct/indirect contact, probability reactivation latent infection transmission parameters. infectious_latent_incuba_period: Infectious period, latent period, incubation period, shape disease period parameters. pathogen_survival: Survival parameters different materials environmental conditions diagnostic_test: Diagnostic test parameters test specificity sensitivity. within_herd_prevalence: Within-herd pathogen prevalence parameters. regional_prevalence: Regional pathogen prevalence parameters country sub-division level. control_plan: Information disease control programs plans implemented different countries. cost_biosecurity: Information prices products needed implement biosecurity measures : Additional parameters information related various infectious diseases animal populations fit specific categories tables aggregated parametra_long. can find full documentation .

title: Livestock Disease Transmission Parameters Database
text: parametra_crossref table contains bibliographic information found Crossref references (“ref”) cited database.

title: Livestock Disease Transmission Parameters Database
text: available excel file: LOT (List Terms): List terms used database meanings. CHANGELOG : Tracks modifications, updates, version history. crossref: Metadata extracted CrossRef using rcrossref package scientific publications referenced parametra_long dataset.

title: Livestock Disease Transmission Parameters Database
text: two ways contribute new parameters PARAMETRA database:

title: Livestock Disease Transmission Parameters Database
text: Visit PARAMETRA submission form Fill required fields single parameter entry Submit form

title: Livestock Disease Transmission Parameters Database
text: Download Excel template Fill Excel template parameter entries, using PARAMETRA database reference proper formatting. Upload file PARAMETRA submission form submissions reviewed PARAMETRA administrators added database ensure data quality consistency.

title: Livestock Disease Transmission Parameters Database
text: contact@parametra.eu directly maintainers: natalia.ciria@uab.cat alistair@kreavet.com

title: Livestock Disease Transmission Parameters Database
text: PARAMETRA developed part BIOSECURE Project, funded European Union’s HORIZON Europe FARM2FORK project. Views opinions expressed however author(s) necessarily reflect European Union REA. Neither European Union granting authority can held responsible .

title: parametra 1.1.0
text: Added cost_biosecurity table, based BioSecure T4.2 biosecurity cost data. Added Sheep Goat pox virus parameters.

title: parametra 1.1.0
text: Renamed columns table names snake_case consistency. Added standardised IDs curated records. Added reviewed column-name definitions README documentation cost_biosecurity table. Automated R data documentation LOT README sheets. Added year column. Added filled_date column track data entry dates. Standardised model type values. Standardised references across PARAMETRA.

title: parametra 1.1.0
text: Implemented R-based curation workflow, including maintainer documentation, read, validate, curate, write Excel database.

title: parametra 1.0.0
text: Initial PARAMETRA release, publication version: https://doi.org/10.1016/j.prevetmed.2025.106668.
